content_en:
———— The Prophetic Medicine =

In addition, Abu Dawood narrated that Ash-Shifaa bint Abdullah said, "The Messenger of Allah came by while I was with Hafsah and said:

"Wliy (Io you not teach her (Hafsah, his wife) the Ruqyah from Namlah as you taught her how to write." An-Namlah, literally, the ant, is called as such because it is a type of swelling that appears on the sides of the body. The Namlah is a known disease and causes a type of sting in the sides that makes one think that an ant is crawling on his side and biting him. There are three types of this disease.

Al-Khallal narrated that Ash-Shifaa bint Abdullah used to recite Ruqyah from the Namlah during the time before Islam. When she emigrated to the Prophet b in Medina, after she had given him her pledge of allegiance in Mecca, she said, "O Messenger of Allah! I used to recite the Ruqyah from the Namlah during the time of jahiliyyah (before Islam) and I want to read it to you." She recited it to him, "In the Name of Allah! May the harm be extracted until it goes back to where it came from without harming anyone. O Allah! Remove the harm, O Lord of the people." She used to recite it on a clean branch of wood seven times, choose a clean place, rub the branch on a clean stone with sour wine vinegar and then anoint the affected area with it. The Hadith In addition asserts the fact that it is legislated to teach women to read

The Prophet's guidance in treating the snake's bite

We mentioned the Hadith in which the Prophet b said,

"There is no Ruqyah more beneficial than one recited for the evil eye and fever."

In addition, Ibn Maj'ah said that A'esha narrated, "The Messenger of Allah b allowed using the Ruqyah for the scorpions and the snakes bite." Further, Jbn Shihab Az-Zuhri narrated, “One of the companions of the Messenger of Allah was stung by a snake. The Prophet sSs said, ‘Is there a person who could recite Ruqyahl!' They said, 'O Messenger of Allah ! The children of AI- ‘Hazm used to recite a Ruqyah for snake's bite but abandoned the practice when you prohibited using . Ruqyah.' He said, ‘Summon 'Umarah bin 'Hazm.' When they summoned him, he recited his Ruqyah to the Prophet who said that there is’ no objection to it. Hence, the Prophet b allowed him to use it, and 'Umarah recited the Rugqyah on the victim." [AI-Bokhary, Muslim, An-Nasa'y and Ahmad].

——— The Prophetic Medicine

The Prophet's _ssidance_in treating an ulcer and

In the Two sound books is narrated that A'esha said:

"Whenever a person would complain from an ulcer (or a canker), or a wound, the Messenger of Allah b would put his finger in the soil (on the ground after inserting it in his mouth, then raise it). saying, In the Name of Allah: with the soil of our earth has got a moisture with the saliva of some of us, let our sick be cured, with our Lord's permission.

This is an easy, beneficial cure for the various ulcers and fresh wounds, especially when there is no available medicine. The sand, which is cold and dry and is available on all parts of the world, dries out the moisture that accumulates around the ulcer or wound. This moisture prevents quick healing by natural means, especially in warm areas. Most ulcers and wounds are In addition accompanied by a hot fever, which, when added to the irritant of warm weather, would only intensify the pain of those who are especially sensitive to heat. Since pure sand is cold and dry, more cooling than even the most effective medications for this purpose, the coolness of the sand will effectively pacify the warmth caused by the wound, especially when the sand is washed and dried. Pure sand In addition helps dry out most harmful

moistures and wetness, because it is a strong drying agent against the wetness that prevents quick healing, as we stated. Further, pure sand helps cool the hotness in the ailing organ, which will strengthen its power of resistance (immunity) and thus end the pain by Allah's Leave,

The Hadith indicates that one should place some saliva on his index finger then place it on pure sand, some of which will stick on to his finger. The finger should then be placed on the affected organ or the wound, and then one recites the supplication mentioned in the Hadith. This practice joins the blessings of mentioning Allah's Name, relating all matters to Him and trusting in Him, adding both the cure and the supplication that will make the remedy even more effective.

There are two opinions regarding the Prophet's statement, "The sand of our land", if whether it entails the sand of the entire earth or just the sand of Medina. There is no doubt that some types of sand are more effective remedies for various ailments than sand from other arcas. Galinus said, "In Alexandria, I saw many people who suffer from sever dropsy and an ailing spleen use the sand of Egypt as ointment on their legs, thighs, hands, backs and ribs, and they used to benefit from this remedy." He In addition said, "Hence, this ointment (muddy sand) could help cure Putrid and limp tumors." He continued, "I know of many people, whose bodies have swollen due to

— The Prophetic Medicine

excessive bleeding, who benefited from remedy using mud. I In addition know of others who used it to cure terminal illnesses that attacked various organs, and the terminal ailments were eradicated." Another author said, "Sand that is imported from the area of Kannus - known. as the island of Mastic - is an effective cleanser and causes the flesh to grow in and around ulcers, thus effectively eradicating them."

Since various types of sand have a healing effect, what about the sand of the best and most blessed area on the face of the earth, especially when mixed with the Prophet's saliva? Added to these blessings is the supplication that the Prophet recited and in which he mentioned the Lord's Name and related all matters to Him. We mentioned that the Rugyah increases in effectiveness according to the strength of faith of the person who is reciting it and the response of the ill person to the Rugqyah. No sane, honorable Muslim doctor can deny this fact. However, if any of these qualities is absent, let such a person say whatever falsehood he wishes!

The Prophet's guidance on treating pain in_ general with Islamic prayer formulas

Muslim [Ibn Ma'jah, Ahmad and At-Tabarani} narrated in his Sahih that ‘Uthman bin Abu AI-'As complained to the Prophet b from a pain in his body that he suffered from ever since he became a Muslim. The

Place your hand on the affected area in your body and say, 'In the Name of Allah,’ thrice. Then say, ‘I seek refuge with Allah's Might and Power from the evil of what I am suffering from, and because of which I have become worry. seven times."

The remedy mentioned here entails reciting Allah's Name, relating all matters to Him and seeking refuge with His Might and Power from the pain, and will surely eradicate the pain. In addition, repeating the supplication contained in the remedy frequently will make it even more effective and successful just as the case when the medication is frequently administered until the disease is fully eradicated.

in the Sahihain it is narrated that the Prophet b used to visit some of his family members (who were ill) and would touch them with his right hand, saying:

"O Allah, che Lord of mankind! Do away with the complaint and bring about the cure. You Alone bring the cure, and there is no cure except Your cure, a cure that does not leave illnesses."

- The last Islamic prayer: formula includes begging Allah by His Perfect Lordship and Mercy to bring about the cure. The Islamic prayer formula In addition affirms

that Allah is the only One Who brings the cure and that there is no cure except the cure that He brings about. The Islamic prayer formula includes begging Allah by His Oneness, Kindness and Lordship.

The Prophet's guidance on treating shock from

Allah said: seo, pt eee, we et Boe eB re Hol Ae 5 HU ot O'ghe aple ELS!

".. but give glad tidings to As-Sabirun (the patient). Who, when afflicted with calamity, say: ‘Truly, to Allah we belong and truly, to Him we shall return. They are those on whom are the Salawat (i.e. who are blessed and will be forgiven) from their Lord, and (they are those who) receive His Mercy, and it is they who are the guided ones." (2:155,157)

In the Musnad (by Imam Ahmad) it is narrated that the Prophet b said:

"No person suffers a calamity and then says, ‘Truly! To Allah we belong and truly, to Him we shall

return. O Allah! Reward me for the calamity that has befallen me and replace it with what is better.’ Except that Allah will reward him for his calamity and will replace it with what is better than whatever he lost."

The words that are contained in the Hadith are a truly effective and are a beneficial cure for those afflicted by disasters or calamities. These words contain two major aspects that if the slaves of Allah acquire knowledge of them, they will relieve them from the shock of the disaster. First, the slave of Allah, his family and wealth are Allah's exclusive property that Allah has loaned to the slave. When Allah takes back some of what he has loaned the slave, He is the Owner who takes back what belongs to Him. Further, whatever Allah gives the save is preceded and followed by periods of non-existence, and the slave only takes temporary possession of them when they come into existence. In addition, the slaves of Allah neither create whatever they own, and they are not its true owners, nor are they capable of protecting such possessions and property. Therefore, the slaves are only hired agents who do what they are commanded, since they are not the real owners.

The slaves return is to Allah, the True Lord, and consequently, they will have to sooner or later depart this life and leave it behind. The slaves will return to Allah just as He created them the first time:

——— The Prophetic Medicine

dispossessed, without a wife or a family or wealth or tribe. Only the good and bad deeds will the slaves have then. If this is the case with whatever Allah gives the slaves, its beginning and end, then why should the slaves be proud of whatever they have or get sad if they loose them? When the slaves remember their beginning and end, this thought will help them discard sadness and depression for their loss.
content_id:
———— Pengobatan Nabawi =

Selain itu, Abu Dawud meriwayatkan bahwa Asy-Syifa binti Abdullah berkata, "Rasulullah datang ketika aku bersama Hafshah dan bersabda:

"Mengapa engkau tidak mengajarinya (Hafshah, istrinya) ruqyah untuk namlah sebagaimana engkau mengajarinya menulis." An-Namlah, secara harfiah berarti semut, disebut demikian karena merupakan jenis pembengkakan yang muncul di sisi-sisi tubuh. Namlah adalah penyakit yang dikenal dan menyebabkan sejenis sengatan di bagian sisi tubuh yang membuat seseorang merasa seolah-olah semut merayap di sisinya dan menggigitnya. Penyakit ini ada tiga jenis.

Al-Khallal meriwayatkan bahwa Asy-Syifa binti Abdullah dahulu membaca ruqyah untuk namlah pada masa sebelum Islam. Ketika beliau berhijrah kepada Nabi di Madinah, setelah memberikan baiatnya di Mekah, beliau berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Dahulu aku membaca ruqyah untuk namlah pada masa jahiliyyah dan aku ingin membacakannya kepadamu." Beliau membacakannya, "Dengan nama Allah! Semoga bahaya itu dicabut hingga kembali ke tempat asalnya tanpa membahayakan siapa pun. Ya Allah, hilangkanlah bahaya ini, wahai Tuhan seluruh manusia." Beliau biasa membacanya pada ranting kayu yang bersih sebanyak tujuh kali, memilih tempat yang bersih, menggosokkan ranting itu pada batu yang bersih dengan cuka anggur asam, kemudian mengoleskannya pada area yang terkena. Hadits ini juga menegaskan fakta bahwa mengajarkan membaca kepada para wanita adalah disyariatkan.

Petunjuk Nabi dalam mengobati gigitan ular

Kami telah menyebutkan hadits di mana Nabi bersabda,

"Tidak ada ruqyah yang lebih bermanfaat daripada yang dibaca untuk ain (mata jahat) dan demam."

Selain itu, Ibnu Majah meriwayatkan bahwa Aisyah berkata, "Rasulullah membolehkan penggunaan ruqyah untuk sengatan kalajengking dan gigitan ular." Lebih lanjut, Ibnu Syihab Az-Zuhri meriwayatkan, "Salah seorang sahabat Rasulullah tersengat ular. Nabi bersabda, 'Adakah seseorang yang bisa membaca ruqyah?' Mereka berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah! Keluarga Al-Hazm dahulu membaca ruqyah untuk gigitan ular, tetapi mereka meninggalkan praktik itu ketika engkau melarang penggunaan ruqyah.' Beliau bersabda, 'Panggillah Umarah bin Hazm.' Ketika mereka memanggilnya, ia membacakan ruqyahnya kepada Nabi yang bersabda bahwa tidak ada keberatan terhadapnya. Maka Nabi membolehkannya menggunakannya, dan Umarah membacakan ruqyah pada korban tersebut." [Al-Bukhari, Muslim, An-Nasa'i dan Ahmad].

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

Petunjuk Nabi dalam mengobati bisul dan luka

Dalam dua kitab shahih diriwayatkan bahwa Aisyah berkata:

"Setiap kali seseorang mengeluhkan bisul (atau sariawan), atau luka, Rasulullah meletakkan jarinya di tanah (di atas tanah setelah memasukkannya ke mulutnya, kemudian mengangkatnya), seraya berkata, 'Dengan nama Allah: dengan tanah bumi kami yang dibasahi dengan air liur sebagian dari kami, semoga orang sakit kami disembuhkan, dengan izin Tuhan kami.'"

Ini adalah pengobatan yang mudah dan bermanfaat untuk berbagai bisul dan luka segar, terutama ketika tidak ada obat yang tersedia. Pasir, yang bersifat dingin dan kering serta tersedia di seluruh penjuru dunia, mengeringkan kelembapan yang menumpuk di sekitar bisul atau luka. Kelembapan ini mencegah penyembuhan cepat secara alami, terutama di daerah yang hangat. Sebagian besar bisul dan luka juga disertai demam panas, yang ketika ditambah dengan panas cuaca yang menyengat, hanya akan memperparah rasa sakit bagi mereka yang sangat sensitif terhadap panas. Karena pasir murni bersifat dingin dan kering, lebih mendinginkan daripada obat-obatan paling efektif sekalipun untuk tujuan ini, kesejukan pasir akan secara efektif meredakan kehangatan yang disebabkan oleh luka, terutama ketika pasir itu dicuci dan dikeringkan. Pasir murni juga membantu mengeringkan sebagian besar

kelembapan dan kebasahan yang berbahaya, karena ia merupakan agen pengering yang kuat melawan kebasahan yang mencegah penyembuhan cepat, sebagaimana telah kami sebutkan. Lebih lanjut, pasir murni membantu mendinginkan kepanasan pada organ yang sakit, yang akan memperkuat daya tahan (imunitas) tubuh dan dengan demikian mengakhiri rasa sakit dengan izin Allah.

Hadits ini menunjukkan bahwa seseorang hendaknya meletakkan sedikit air liur pada jari telunjuknya kemudian meletakkannya di atas pasir murni, yang sebagiannya akan menempel pada jarinya. Jari tersebut kemudian diletakkan pada organ yang terkena atau luka, lalu membaca du'a yang disebutkan dalam hadits. Praktik ini menggabungkan keberkahan menyebut nama Allah, menyandarkan semua urusan kepada-Nya dan bertawakkal kepada-Nya, menambahkan obat dan du'a yang akan membuat pengobatan itu semakin efektif.

Ada dua pendapat mengenai sabda Nabi, "Tanah bumi kami", apakah yang dimaksud adalah tanah seluruh bumi atau hanya tanah Madinah. Tidak diragukan bahwa beberapa jenis tanah lebih efektif sebagai obat untuk berbagai penyakit daripada tanah dari daerah lain. Galenus berkata, "Di Aleksandria, aku melihat banyak orang yang menderita busung air parah dan limpa yang sakit menggunakan pasir Mesir sebagai salep pada kaki, paha, tangan, punggung dan rusuk mereka, dan mereka biasa mendapatkan manfaat dari pengobatan ini." Ia juga berkata, "Oleh karena itu, salep ini (pasir berlumpur) bisa membantu menyembuhkan tumor yang busuk dan lembek." Ia melanjutkan, "Aku mengetahui banyak orang, yang tubuhnya membengkak karena

— Pengobatan Nabawi

pendarahan berlebihan, yang mendapat manfaat dari pengobatan menggunakan lumpur. Aku juga mengetahui orang lain yang menggunakannya untuk menyembuhkan penyakit terminal yang menyerang berbagai organ, dan penyakit terminal itu berhasil diberantas." Penulis lain berkata, "Pasir yang diimpor dari daerah Kannus — yang dikenal sebagai pulau Mastik — adalah pembersih yang efektif dan menyebabkan daging tumbuh di dalam dan sekitar bisul, sehingga secara efektif memberantasnya."

Karena berbagai jenis pasir memiliki efek penyembuhan, bagaimana dengan pasir dari daerah yang terbaik dan paling diberkahi di muka bumi, terutama ketika dicampurkan dengan air liur Nabi? Ditambah dengan keberkahan-keberkahan ini adalah du'a yang dibacakan Nabi dan di dalamnya beliau menyebut nama Tuhan dan menyandarkan semua urusan kepada-Nya. Kami telah menyebutkan bahwa ruqyah semakin bertambah efektif sesuai dengan kekuatan iman orang yang membacanya dan respons orang sakit terhadap ruqyah tersebut. Tidak ada dokter Muslim yang waras dan terhormat yang bisa menyangkal fakta ini. Namun, jika salah satu dari kualitas-kualitas ini tidak ada, biarlah orang seperti itu berkata apa pun kebatilan yang dia inginkan!

Petunjuk Nabi dalam mengobati rasa sakit secara umum dengan ruqyah syar'iyyah

Muslim [Ibnu Majah, Ahmad dan Ath-Thabarani] meriwayatkan dalam Shahihnya bahwa Utsman bin Abu Al-Ash mengadukan kepada Nabi tentang rasa sakit di tubuhnya yang dideritanya sejak ia masuk Islam. Nabi bersabda:

"Letakkan tanganmu pada bagian yang sakit di tubuhmu dan ucapkan, 'Bismillah (Dengan nama Allah),' tiga kali. Kemudian ucapkan, 'Aku berlindung dengan kemuliaan dan kekuasaan Allah dari kejahatan apa yang aku derita, dan yang membuatku khawatir,' tujuh kali."

Pengobatan yang disebutkan di sini meliputi membaca nama Allah, menyandarkan semua urusan kepada-Nya dan berlindung dengan Kemuliaan dan Kekuasaan-Nya dari rasa sakit, dan niscaya akan memberantas rasa sakit tersebut. Selain itu, mengulang du'a yang terkandung dalam pengobatan ini secara sering akan membuatnya semakin efektif dan berhasil, sebagaimana halnya ketika obat diberikan secara terus-menerus hingga penyakit benar-benar diberantas.

Dalam Shahihain diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi biasa mengunjungi sebagian anggota keluarganya (yang sakit) dan mengusap mereka dengan tangan kanannya, seraya berkata:

"Ya Allah, Tuhan seluruh manusia! Hilangkanlah penyakit ini dan datangkanlah kesembuhan. Engkau satu-satunya yang mendatangkan kesembuhan, dan tidak ada kesembuhan kecuali kesembuhan dari-Mu, kesembuhan yang tidak menyisakan penyakit."

Ruqyah terakhir ini mengandung permohonan kepada Allah dengan Rububiyyah dan Rahmat-Nya yang Sempurna untuk mendatangkan kesembuhan. Ruqyah ini juga menegaskan

bahwa Allah adalah satu-satunya yang mendatangkan kesembuhan dan tidak ada kesembuhan kecuali kesembuhan yang Dia datangkan. Ruqyah ini mengandung permohonan kepada Allah dengan Keesaan, Kebaikan dan Rububiyyah-Nya.

Petunjuk Nabi dalam mengobati guncangan akibat musibah

Allah berfirman:

"...tetapi berikanlah kabar gembira kepada orang-orang yang sabar. Yaitu orang-orang yang apabila ditimpa musibah, mereka mengucapkan: 'Sesungguhnya kami milik Allah dan kepada-Nya kami kembali.' Mereka itulah yang mendapat shalawat (keberkahan dan ampunan) dari Tuhan mereka, dan (mereka itulah yang) mendapat rahmat-Nya, dan mereka itulah orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk." (QS. Al-Baqarah: 155-157)

Dalam Musnad (Imam Ahmad) diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bersabda:

"Tidaklah seseorang ditimpa musibah kemudian mengucapkan, 'Sesungguhnya kami milik Allah dan kepada-Nya kami kembali,

kembali. Ya Allah! Berilah aku pahala atas musibah yang menimpaku dan gantikanlah dengan yang lebih baik.' Kecuali Allah akan memberinya pahala atas musibahnya dan menggantinya dengan sesuatu yang lebih baik dari apa yang hilang darinya."

Kata-kata yang terkandung dalam hadith tersebut benar-benar mujarab dan merupakan obat yang bermanfaat bagi mereka yang tertimpa bencana atau musibah. Kata-kata ini mengandung dua aspek utama yang jika para hamba Allah memahaminya, akan meringankan mereka dari guncangan musibah tersebut. Pertama, hamba Allah, keluarganya, dan hartanya adalah milik Allah semata yang Allah pinjamkan kepada hamba-Nya. Ketika Allah mengambil kembali sebagian dari apa yang Dia pinjamkan kepada hamba-Nya, Dia adalah Pemilik yang mengambil kembali apa yang menjadi milik-Nya. Lebih jauh lagi, apa pun yang Allah berikan kepada hamba-Nya didahului dan diikuti oleh masa ketiadaan, dan hamba hanya memilikinya sementara ketika semua itu ada. Selain itu, para hamba Allah tidak menciptakan apa pun yang mereka miliki, dan mereka bukanlah pemilik sejatinya, juga tidak mampu melindungi harta dan kepemilikan tersebut. Oleh karena itu, para hamba hanyalah wakil yang ditugaskan untuk melaksanakan apa yang diperintahkan, karena mereka bukanlah pemilik yang sesungguhnya.

Kepulangan para hamba adalah kepada Allah, Tuhan Yang Maha Benar, dan karenanya mereka cepat atau lambat harus meninggalkan kehidupan ini dan meninggalkannya di belakang. Para hamba akan kembali kepada Allah sebagaimana Dia menciptakan mereka pertama kali:

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

tanpa memiliki apa pun, tanpa istri, tanpa keluarga, tanpa harta, dan tanpa kabilah. Hanya amal baik dan buruklah yang akan dimiliki para hamba saat itu. Jika demikianlah keadaan apa pun yang Allah berikan kepada para hamba, awal dan akhirnya, maka mengapa para hamba harus bangga dengan apa yang mereka miliki atau bersedih jika kehilangannya? Ketika para hamba mengingat awal dan akhir mereka, pemikiran ini akan membantu mereka menanggalkan kesedihan dan kemurungan atas kehilangan mereka.
content_ar:
———— The Prophetic Medicine =

In addition, Abu Dawood narrated that Ash-Shifaa bint Abdullah said, "رسول الله ﷺ came by while I was with Hafsah and said:

"Wliy (Io you not teach her (Hafsah, his wife) the Ruqyah from Namlah as you taught her how to write." An-Namlah, literally, the ant, is called as such because it is a type of swelling that appears on the sides of the body. The Namlah is a known disease and causes a type of sting in the sides that makes one think that an ant is crawling on his side and biting him. There are three types of this disease.

Al-Khallal narrated that Ash-Shifaa bint Abdullah used to recite Ruqyah from the Namlah during the time before Islam. When she emigrated to النبي ﷺ b in Medina, after she had given him her pledge of allegiance in Mecca, she said, "O رسول الله ﷺ! I used to recite the Ruqyah from the Namlah during the time of jahiliyyah (before Islam) and I want to read it to you." She recited it to him, "In the Name of الله! May the harm be extracted until it goes back to where it came from without harming anyone. O الله! Remove the harm, O Lord of the people." She used to recite it on a clean branch of wood seven times, choose a clean place, rub the branch on a clean stone with sour wine vinegar and then anoint the affected area with it. The الحديث In addition asserts the fact that it is legislated to teach women to read

النبي ﷺ's guidance in treating the snake's bite

We mentioned the الحديث in which النبي ﷺ b said,

"There is no Ruqyah more beneficial than one recited for the evil eye and fever."

In addition, Ibn Maj'ah said that A'esha narrated, "رسول الله ﷺ b allowed using the Ruqyah for the scorpions and the snakes bite." Further, Jbn Shihab Az-Zuhri narrated, “One of the الصحابة of رسول الله ﷺ was stung by a snake. النبي ﷺ sSs said, ‘Is there a person who could recite Ruqyahl!' They said, 'O رسول الله ﷺ ! The children of AI- ‘Hazm used to recite a Ruqyah for snake's bite but abandoned the practice when you prohibited using . Ruqyah.' He said, ‘Summon 'Umarah bin 'Hazm.' When they summoned him, he recited his Ruqyah to النبي ﷺ who said that there is’ no objection to it. Hence, النبي ﷺ b allowed him to use it, and 'Umarah recited the Rugqyah on the victim." [AI-Bokhary, Muslim, An-Nasa'y and Ahmad].

——— The Prophetic Medicine

النبي ﷺ's _ssidance_in treating an ulcer and

In the Two sound books is narrated that A'esha said:

"Whenever a person would complain from an ulcer (or a canker), or a wound, رسول الله ﷺ b would put his finger in the soil (on the ground after inserting it in his mouth, then raise it). saying, In the Name of الله: with the soil of our earth has got a moisture with the saliva of some of us, let our sick be cured, with our Lord's permission.

This is an easy, beneficial cure for the various ulcers and fresh wounds, especially when there is no available medicine. The sand, which is cold and dry and is available on all parts of the world, dries out the moisture that accumulates around the ulcer or wound. This moisture prevents quick healing by natural means, especially in warm areas. Most ulcers and wounds are In addition accompanied by a hot fever, which, when added to the irritant of warm weather, would only intensify the pain of those who are especially sensitive to heat. Since pure sand is cold and dry, more cooling than even the most effective medications for this purpose, the coolness of the sand will effectively pacify the warmth caused by the wound, especially when the sand is washed and dried. Pure sand In addition helps dry out most harmful

moistures and wetness, because it is a strong drying agent against the wetness that prevents quick healing, as we stated. Further, pure sand helps cool the hotness in the ailing organ, which will strengthen its power of resistance (immunity) and thus end the pain by الله's Leave,

The الحديث indicates that one should place some saliva on his index finger then place it on pure sand, some of which will stick on to his finger. The finger should then be placed on the affected organ or the wound, and then one recites the supplication mentioned in the الحديث. This practice joins the blessings of mentioning الله's Name, relating all matters to Him and trusting in Him, adding both the cure and the supplication that will make the remedy even more effective.

There are two opinions regarding النبي ﷺ's statement, "The sand of our land", if whether it entails the sand of the entire earth or just the sand of Medina. There is no doubt that some types of sand are more effective remedies for various ailments than sand from other arcas. Galinus said, "In Alexandria, I saw many people who suffer from sever dropsy and an ailing spleen use the sand of Egypt as ointment on their legs, thighs, hands, backs and ribs, and they used to benefit from this remedy." He In addition said, "Hence, this ointment (muddy sand) could help cure Putrid and limp tumors." He continued, "I know of many people, whose bodies have swollen due to

— The Prophetic Medicine

excessive bleeding, who benefited from remedy using mud. I In addition know of others who used it to cure terminal illnesses that attacked various organs, and the terminal ailments were eradicated." Another author said, "Sand that is imported from the area of Kannus - known. as the island of Mastic - is an effective cleanser and causes the flesh to grow in and around ulcers, thus effectively eradicating them."

Since various types of sand have a healing effect, what about the sand of the best and most blessed area on the face of the earth, especially when mixed with النبي ﷺ's saliva? Added to these blessings is the supplication that النبي ﷺ recited and in which he mentioned the Lord's Name and related all matters to Him. We mentioned that the Rugyah increases in effectiveness according to the strength of faith of the person who is reciting it and the response of the ill person to the Rugqyah. No sane, honorable Muslim doctor can deny this fact. However, if any of these qualities is absent, let such a person say whatever falsehood he wishes!

النبي ﷺ's guidance on treating pain in_ general with Islamic prayer formulas

Muslim [Ibn Ma'jah, Ahmad and At-Tabarani} narrated in his Sahih that ‘Uthman bin Abu AI-'As complained to النبي ﷺ b from a pain in his body that he suffered from ever since he became a Muslim. The

Place your hand on the affected area in your body and say, 'In the Name of الله,’ thrice. Then say, ‘I seek refuge with الله's Might and Power from the evil of what I am suffering from, and because of which I have become worry. seven times."

The remedy mentioned here entails reciting الله's Name, relating all matters to Him and seeking refuge with His Might and Power from the pain, and will surely eradicate the pain. In addition, repeating the supplication contained in the remedy frequently will make it even more effective and successful just as the case when the medication is frequently administered until the disease is fully eradicated.

in the Sahihain it is narrated that النبي ﷺ b used to visit some of his family members (who were ill) and would touch them with his right hand, saying:

"O الله, che Lord of mankind! Do away with the complaint and bring about the cure. You Alone bring the cure, and there is no cure except Your cure, a cure that does not leave illnesses."

- The last Islamic prayer: formula includes begging الله by His Perfect Lordship and Mercy to bring about the cure. The Islamic prayer formula In addition affirms

that الله is the only One Who brings the cure and that there is no cure except the cure that He brings about. The Islamic prayer formula includes begging الله by His Oneness, Kindness and Lordship.

النبي ﷺ's guidance on treating shock from

الله said: seo, pt eee, we et Boe eB re Hol Ae 5 HU ot O'ghe aple ELS!

".. but give glad tidings to As-Sabirun (the patient). Who, when afflicted with calamity, say: ‘Truly, to الله we belong and truly, to Him we shall return. They are those on whom are the Salawat (i.e. who are blessed and will be forgiven) from their Lord, and (they are those who) receive His Mercy, and it is they who are the guided ones." (2:155,157)

In the Musnad (by Imam Ahmad) it is narrated that النبي ﷺ b said:

"No person suffers a calamity and then says, ‘Truly! To الله we belong and truly, to Him we shall

return. O الله! Reward me for the calamity that has befallen me and replace it with what is better.’ Except that الله will reward him for his calamity and will replace it with what is better than whatever he lost."

The words that are contained in the الحديث are a truly effective and are a beneficial cure for those afflicted by disasters or calamities. These words contain two major aspects that if the slaves of الله acquire knowledge of them, they will relieve them from the shock of the disaster. First, the slave of الله, his family and wealth are الله's exclusive property that الله has loaned to the slave. When الله takes back some of what he has loaned the slave, He is the Owner who takes back what belongs to Him. Further, whatever الله gives the save is preceded and followed by periods of non-existence, and the slave only takes temporary possession of them when they come into existence. In addition, the slaves of الله neither create whatever they own, and they are not its true owners, nor are they capable of protecting such possessions and property. Therefore, the slaves are only hired agents who do what they are commanded, since they are not the real owners.

The slaves return is to الله, the True Lord, and consequently, they will have to sooner or later depart this life and leave it behind. The slaves will return to الله just as He created them the first time:

——— The Prophetic Medicine

dispossessed, without a wife or a family or wealth or tribe. Only the good and bad deeds will the slaves have then. If this is the case with whatever الله gives the slaves, its beginning and end, then why should the slaves be proud of whatever they have or get sad if they loose them? When the slaves remember their beginning and end, this thought will help them discard sadness and depression for their loss.
